title.text: Chapter 15: Security for IoT - Common Challenges in OT Security

line.data-narration: Protects operational technology systems, including sensors, actuators, and control systems, from cyber threats.

text.data-narration: Lack of standardization makes it difficult to implement security measures.

line.data-narration: Custom-built systems lack standardization, making it challenging to develop common security protocols and guidelines.

text.data-narration: Insufficient security measures make OT systems vulnerable to cyber attacks.

line.data-narration: Firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and encryption are often not implemented or not designed with security in mind, making these systems vulnerable to cyber attacks.

text.data-narration: Limited visibility and monitoring capabilities make it difficult to detect and respond to security incidents.

line.data-narration: OT systems often lack visibility and monitoring capabilities, making it challenging to identify and address security vulnerabilities.

text.data-narration: Interoperability issues make it difficult to integrate security measures across systems.

line.data-narration: OT systems from different vendors can have interoperability issues, making it challenging to integrate security measures across systems.
